item.title: The Logistics Of Living In A Treehouse..... We finished the uppe...
item.description:
The Logistics Of Living In A Treehouse..... We finished the upper level of the treehouse in January, so the porch turniture was way overdue. The sun does start to beat on the porch around 3-4pm but most of hte day its wonderful up there, esepcially with the fan and the breeze coming off the pond. Its a great place to watch birds dive bomb the pond.
#treehouse #diy #fyp #tinyhouse #build
FULL POST | EMBED | REVERSED

item.title: Absolutely nerve racking to cut up irreplaceable pieces of wood....
item.description:
Absolutely nerve racking to cut up irreplaceable pieces of wood....carpenters know the feeling. My vote was for a black sink to match the appliances, but the wife said nope, I want a white farmhouse style sink so thats what we did.
#treehouse #DIY #fyp #tinyhouse #build

FULL POST | EMBED | REVERSED

item.title: These look AMAZING! A couple things I expect people comment abou...
item.description:
These look AMAZING! A couple things I expect people comment about… The big “cracks” are not really cracks, but spacing left behind where a branch and trunk fused together decades ago. The slabs are kiln dried and acclimated, they’re not going to crack more I’ve never done bowtie joints before and I don’t have the proper tools to do it. It wasn’t worth the risk of screwing up the irreplaceable counters. It’s been 3 weeks as of posting this and they look perfect, no changes in the wood and the epoxy fills look 10/10 • 5. You may notice I did not remove the bark from inside the cracks. The bark has been compressed inside the tree for decades and might as well have been welded on, I hammered, chisled and pried and it absolutely did not budge. So I said “fine, bark, if you wanna stay just stay, you look cool anyway” We’ll see if that ends up being a mistake but I think that
#treehouse #DIY #fyp #tinyhouse #build
FULL POST | EMBED | REVERSED

item.title: in 2024 we had the property logged, and this tree had lost 1/2 o...
item.description:
in 2024 we had the property logged, and this tree had lost 1/2 of its remaning branches that morning, and it needed to come down,so I aksed the loggers to push it down. Two massive logging machines attacked the tree for 10-15 minutes before they could get it down, the old beast put up a fight. it had rot vertically down the entire side of the trunk and I thought it was going to be hollow and just good for firewood. Well turns out it was mostly not hollow, and the trunk was up to 60" in diameter where the branches forked off. I realized I needed to get this log moved, and milled, both of which were going to be difficult tasks. I called very sawmil I knew and none of them could handle a log of this size, until I got in touch with a guy who could do it, but said it would be atleast $5000 to come on site and mill it. That wasn't worth it, so while the loggers were still there, they loaded the main trunk of this tree onto my trailer and I drove it an hour away to a sawmill that could handle it. When I got there, it took 2 forklifts to lift it off hte trailer enough that I could drive out from under. The log weighed 15,000 pounds. My trailer was only rated for 14,000. My truck was only rated for 10,000. All together I ended up hauling a 20,000lb with my 10,000lb limit truck. 6 months later I picked up the slabs, and stacked them under a tarp for a year to slowy air dry, before loading them back up and taking them to another sawmill where they were kiln dried, and then planed. After that I had to get them up into the treehouse, which as you can see here is quite difficult. It's been immense amounts of work to get these countertops in place, much more off camera and behind the scenes then you see here, but its worth it, and its from the property. Same as the cedar siding.
#treehouse #diy #tinyhouse #fyp #build
FULL POST | EMBED | REVERSED

item.title: WE'RE BACK! Im looking forward to being back on my treehouse bui...
item.description:
WE'RE BACK! Im looking forward to being back on my treehouse build, but there is still a TON of work to do. Im really not sure how they build houses in 2-3 months even with a crew. Being on the ground probably helps. But all these details really take a lot of time. Im ready to be done, but the content is gonna be PEAK! Stay tuned!
#treehouse #diy #tinyhouse #fyp #build
FULL POST | EMBED | REVERSED
